one_liner: NFLX +4.66% to $77.65 — new watchlist addition rallying on $25B buyback expansion and pre-Q2-earnings momentum (July 16); DCF shows 71.5% upside and the ad revenue ramp is real.

price_action.intraday_story: NFLX is the new addition to the watchlist today. The 90-day max-volume day was Feb 27 (2.1x) — a potential prior SC. Today's 1.28x volume move to $77.65 approaches the top of the recent $65-78 range (max $78.44). The close near the high on above-average volume is constructive. The $74.91 intraday dip was quickly absorbed. Key risk: Q2 earnings July 16 could create a buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news dyn

news_catalyst.headline: Netflix $25B buyback expansion + Q2 earnings July 16 + ad revenue above $1.5B targeting $3B
news_catalyst.summary: Netflix expanded its share repurchase authorization to $25B (adding to an existing $15B program) while the stock trades 30-35% below recent highs. Management's buyback signal at these levels is a strong insider buy indicator. Q2 earnings due July 16 are expected to show continued subscriber strength from live sports (NFL, WWE, F1), ad-tier growth, and gaming. Ad revenue already surpassing $1.5B with a $3B 2026 target represents a new high-margin revenue stream the market has not fully incorporated into DCF models.

technical.summary: NFLX is the new addition to the watchlist today. The 90-day max-volume day was Feb 27 (2.1x) — a potential prior SC. Today's 1.28x volume move to $77.65 approaches the top of the recent $65-78 range (max $78.44). The close near the high on above-average volume is constructive. The $74.91 intraday dip was quickly absorbed. Key risk: Q2 earnings July 16 could create a buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news dynamic like TSLA today. DCF at $272.5 vs $77.65 = 71.5% MoS is the most compelling fundamental setup in the watchlist.

wyckoff_narrative: Insufficient history for full Wyckoff read as a new watchlist addition. Consolidation in $65-78 range with today's 1.28x volume move toward the top has SOS characteristics. Watch for break above $78.44 on volume to confirm the start of markup. $74.91 is now near-term support.

valuation.summary: DCF $272.5 vs $77.65 = 71.5% MoS — highest in the watchlist. Netflix's transition to profitability (ad tier, password crackdown, live sports) may make the DCF model conservative. A stock at 28% of estimated fair value with an active buyback is a compelling setup.

risks: Q2 earnings July 16: buy-the-rumor-sell-the-news risk after +4.66% pre-earnings move
$78.44 is top of recent range — overhead supply at the resistance level
New addition — limited Wyckoff history to establish phase clarity
